name: Binary Tree Longest Consecutive Sequence II

similar_questions.title: Check if an Array Is Consecutive

title: Binary Tree Longest Consecutive Sequence II

discussion_posts.name: WilmerKrisp
discussion_posts.content:
<--please vote

- at each step I have to find out whether the previous left/right branch was increasing/decreasing
- in order to continue the path higher, I take the maximum piece lower (whether it was increasing or decreasing, whether it was on the left or on the right)
- - if, for example, the branch on the left ceases to increase, then the counter of the left-increment is reset to one
, the horizontal path consists of the word of the increasing and decreasing piece on the right, then the sum of the increment and decrement is smaller



![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/8c2e347b-d9d3-48d6-bfcd-41d7dadd5c8c_1641461946.7102733.png)

![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/6d83ff17-8283-4085-8c5d-6ab23cc1be08_1641461951.6068883.png)

discussion_posts.name: jmzhang18
discussion_posts.content:
As the tree visualization, 

			1
		/        \
	   2            3
	 /
	 4
Isn't the longest path [1,2,4], the answer should be 3, why the correct answer is 2?
